completeness_checks.note: All 62 EU AI Act elements evaluated (8 competency, 54 obligation). Each element scored using competency_implication text against WIDAI KSA pool. Both obligation_text and competency_implication preserved in every rationale file.

rationale_documentation.note: Every rationale file validated as well-formed JSON with complete method coverage. All 62 files contain STS primary + 4 secondary methods (BERTScore, NLI, Bi-Encoder, Jaccard) with content-specific significance text. Each file preserves dual provenance (obligation_text + competency_implication).

relationship_type_consistency.subset_of_note: No 'Subset of' relationships. Expected: EU AI Act competency implications are regulatory requirements scoped to specific obligations — they are equal to or broader than individual WIDAI KSAs, never narrower.
relationship_type_consistency.no_relationship_note: Zero no-relationship results. This is the expected outcome for a regulatory framework whose workforce requirements were considered during WIDAI KSA pool enrichment (Phase 1A). It indicates comprehensive coverage of EU AI Act workforce requirements by the WIDAI pool, not an artifact of permissive classification thresholds.
relationship_type_consistency.strength_mapping_consistency_check: PASS — Equal (mean 7.17) > Superset (mean 5.88) > Intersects (mean 4.3). Monotonically decreasing strength across relationship types, consistent with all four prior STRMs.

strength_scoring.scoring_methodology: Cross-Encoder STS (stsb-roberta-base) as primary for all elements, raw 0-1 mapped to 0-10 integer. Functional rationale per NIST IR 8477.
strength_scoring.element_type_consistency: PASS — Competency (mean 6.31) and obligation (mean 6.35) elements score nearly identically. This confirms the competency_implication field successfully bridges the vocabulary gap between legal prose and competency language. If the gap were not bridged, obligation elements would score systematically lower.

mapping_strength_rationale_consistency.note: All strength values in strm_mapping.json match corresponding rationale files.

comparison_to_prior_strms.note: STRM-005 scored mean (6.34) is higher than prior STRMs (4.18–5.12). This is expected: EU AI Act competency_implication text was authored to describe workforce competencies implied by regulatory obligations — the text is naturally closer to competency language than raw framework element descriptions (NICE KSATs, O*NET DWAs, etc.). Additionally, the EU AI Act scope (62 elements from 21 articles) is focused specifically on data/AI governance — the core of WIDAI's domain — whereas prior STRMs included many elements outside WIDAI scope.
comparison_to_prior_strms.consistency_assessment: CONSISTENT — Higher mean is methodologically explained, not an artifact of scoring drift.

nli_diagnostic.description: NLI entailment was initially planned as primary for obligation elements. Diagnostic scoring revealed near-zero signal.
nli_diagnostic.conclusion: NLI entailment measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Competency→competency pairs are 'neutral' in NLI terms. STS provides well-distributed, actionable signal. Pipeline revised to STS-primary for all elements.
nli_diagnostic.status: DOCUMENTED — Methodology evolution recorded in use_case.json, scoring_summary.json, and ADR-019

rationale_type_validation.justification: Mapping direction is obligation→competency (what workforce capability fulfills this obligation?). This is a Functional question per NIST IR 8477. Prior STRMs mapped competency↔competency (Semantic).
rationale_type_validation.status: PASS — Rationale type deviation justified by element type difference

per_fde_detection.criteria: NLI domain mismatch, STS threshold fragility, bi-encoder vocabulary inflation

gap_analysis.note: Original assessment reported zero gaps — this was incorrect due to hardcoded null gap_signal in the scoring pipeline. Retroactive gap detection found 24 elements with gap signals (11 critical, 8 moderate, 5 low). The dominant pattern is AG-domain vocabulary inflation: regulatory language shares surface tokens with governance KSAs, causing the bi-encoder candidate selection to systematically prefer AG matches over functionally correct KSAs from DQ, AI, TF, and SP domains. All 62 elements still map to at least one WIDAI KSA with strength ≥ 4, but 11 of those matches are in the wrong functional domain.
gap_analysis.status: CORRECTED — 24 gap signals detected retroactively. Original PASS was incorrect.

cross_strm_consistency.rationale_type_deviation: Functional (vs. Semantic in STRM-001–004). Justified by regulation→competency mapping direction.
cross_strm_consistency.scoring_method_note: STS-primary for all elements, same as STRM-001–004. NLI-primary was planned, tested, found inappropriate, and documented.
cross_strm_consistency.distribution_note: STRM-005 has 0% No relationship vs. 0.3% (STRM-001), 9.5% (STRM-002), 0.3% (STRM-003), 0% (STRM-004). Zero no-relationship is consistent with STRM-004 and explained by the narrow, in-domain scope of the EU AI Act elements.

overall_status: CORRECTED — 6/7 checks pass, 1 corrected (gap_analysis). Original 7/7 PASS was incorrect.
correction_note: Gap analysis originally reported PASS with zero gaps. Retroactive multi-method gap detection (2026-04-01) found 24 elements with gap signals including 11 critical domain mismatches. Gap register and rationale files updated. All other QA/QC checks remain valid.
